question: Comment tracer la provenance et maintenir un audit trail du code genere par agent IA ?

principles.text: Maintenir un audit trail structure par session d'agent : model name + version, prompt, tool calls, cost, final diff, lie au git commit via trailer metadata (ex: Agent-Logs-Url style GitHub Copilot). Le simple Co-Authored-By trailer est insuffisant pour compliance (pas de cryptographic signature, pas de contenu queryable). Pour contextes regles (SOC2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, ISO 42001 certification), escalade obligatoire vers **STANDARD** : audit trail structured + queryable + immutable. Full session replay est CHOIX d'equipe (storage cost tradeoff). Outils emergents : git-ai (OSS) + GitHub Copilot Agent-Logs-Url + CodeSlick (vendor).

sources.name: GitHub Blog 2026-03-20 - Trace any Copilot coding agent commit to its session logs
sources.quote: the agent's commits link back to the agent session logs by including an Agent-Logs-Url trailer in the commit message

sources.name: ISO/IEC 5338:2023 §6.3.6 Information management + §6.3.7 Measurement (sous Technical management processes)
Kothari - Claude Code SOC 2 compliance practitioner guide
git-ai-project/git-ai - Git extension for AI-generated code tracking

plus: important-effect (production implementation exists, SOC2 compliance gap)

etd_balance.benefices: Tracabilite incidents, conformite SOC 2 + EU AI Act Art 12 + ISO 42001
etd_balance.risques: Volume logs, PII leak dans traces, cout storage
